subtitle: Shin or calf — location not pinpointed

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Lower leg pain is often the first warning sign of a mileage spike

mild.description: General lower leg aching or tightness after runs
mild.can_run_note: Yes — try to identify whether it is shin or calf
why: General lower leg fatigue from accumulated training load
Multiple structures under stress from mileage increase
Poor running footwear or surface change
relief: Ice lower leg after every run
Calf stretching — both straight and bent knee versions
prevention: Use this app to identify shin vs calf for specific advice

moderate.description: Lower leg pain affecting your running
moderate.can_run_note: Easy runs on soft surfaces only
why: Possible early stress reaction in the tibia
relief: Reduce mileage by 50% and run only on soft surfaces
Identify specific location — use the app to narrow down

severe.description: Significant lower leg pain limiting walking
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible stress fracture
why: Possible tibial or fibular stress fracture requiring imaging
relief: Rest and see a physiotherapist or GP urgently
Imaging to rule out stress fracture
prevention: Return to running only with clear diagnosis and structured plan

see_physio_if: A specific very tender spot on the bone
No improvement after 5 days of rest
